title: Exhaust vent
abstract: An exhaust vent is provided including a first portion inserted through an opening in an outer wall of a structure for fluidly connecting to an appliance to be vented within the structure, a second portion fluidly connected to the first portion at a junction and having an open end opening into the atmosphere, a flap disposed at the junction configured to open by pressure from exhaust air directed through the first portion from the appliance and allow the exhaust air to pass through the second portion and be vented through the open end to the atmosphere, and the flap is further configured to close by gravity to seal the junction between the first and second portions when no exhaust air is directed through the first portion from the appliance to prevent back airflow from the atmosphere through the open end through the second portion to the first portion.
claims: 1. An exhaust vent, comprising: a first portion inserted through an opening in an outer wall of a structure for fluidly connecting to an appliance to be vented within the structure and having an upper sidewall extending an elongated distance from the outer wall and a lower sidewall; a second portion fluidly and non-removably connected to the first portion exterior to the outer wall and having an open end downwardly directed and opening into the atmosphere, said first and second portions defining a duct, and said second portion further having at least one substantially elongated exclusively planar rear sidewall that extends from a lower sidewall of the first portion and abuts and parallels the outer wall to the open end of the second portion and an exclusively planar front sidewall spaced apart from and parallel to the rear sidewall; a flap disposed within the duct normally in a closed position configured to move to an open position by pressure from exhaust air directed through the first portion from the appliance and allow the exhaust air to pass through the second portion and be vented through the open end to the atmosphere; and the rear sidewall of the second portion and the lower sidewall of the first portion are joined together and oriented perpendicularly collectively forming an elongated edge within the duct and in the closed position the flap obliquely rests at a medial point against the edge to seal the duct; wherein an upper end of the flap is pivotally connected to the second portion of the duct within the outer wall and an opposite end is disposed within the second portion exterior to the outer wall.
